channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Pedestrian stud faded on D phase- required repainting Stop line faded on A, B phase - required repainting, 14th March
item.description:
Pedestrian stud faded on D phase- required repainting Stop line faded on A, B phase - required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43, Friern Barnet Road, Brunswick Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Timetables for routes 227 and 367, Bromley-bound, are missing from the bus stop flag panel, bus stop E, opp 3, Bromley Rd, Beckenham nr St George's Church and the jct with St George's Rd., 14th March
item.description:
Timetables for routes 227 and 367, Bromley-bound, are missing from the bus stop flag panel, bus stop E, opp 3, Bromley Rd, Beckenham nr St George's Church and the jct with St George's Rd. Timetables for the 162 and N3 are on display

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Georges Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littering on verge along Crossway, 14th March
item.description:
There is constant excessive littering on the verge on the south side of Crossway, attracting vermin. The verge on the north side of Crossway closer to Boleyn road is fenced up and much tidier. Could someone do the same to the south side road verge?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Crossway, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Handel street, 14th March
item.description:
I would like to submit a report for vehicle damage caused by a pothole on Handel Street, WC1. The incident occurred on 13/03/26 while I was driving my Mercedes C180 Kompressor (registration GV58GZM). The vehicle struck a pothole in the road which caused damage to the steering/suspension, leaving the front wheel misaligned and the car undriveable.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Handel Street, King's Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lighting keeps flashing, 14th March
item.description:
Street lamp keeps flashing off and on constant all night and its causing a nausance through my bedroom window as well as making this area unlit and unsafe.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selden Road, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The A10 River, 14th March
item.description:
I know I am not the first to report this. This is a known issue and it has been ongoing for well over a decade. There is a serious flooding problem at this dipped section of the A10. Water naturally collects at the lowest point, and that is exactly what happens here time and time again. This now needs proper attention from skilled engineers to deliver a permanent solution, not another temporary fix. A proper engineering review is clearly needed, whether that involves increasing the drainage capacity, installing additional gullies at the lowest point, reshaping the road surface so water flows away more effectively, or implementing a pumped drainage system so water cannot continue to collect in this dip. This has been an ongoing problem for far too long and it now requires a permanent, engineered solution rather than repeated short term responses.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Great Cambridge Road, Southbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Globe light uniluminated on junction of Selby Road with Seymore Villas, 14th March
item.description:
Column number C738, on traffic island here

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Selby Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on TWO illuminated bollards, 14th March
item.description:
On traffic island halfway down Seymore villas, in the middle of the road, there is black pen graffiti on TWO plastic bollards. Graffiti tag depicts "oops" Please see to photos attached.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Seymour Villas, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grafiti on blue signs on BOTH sides of traffic island, 14th March
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Selby Road, Anerley, Greater London

item.title: This pothole is a deep crater occupying the whole of the middle of the roundabout, making it impossible to avoid. The wear and tear on my car in your Borough is ridiculous , please please get out and fix everything!, 14th March
item.description:
Been here for ages and is awfully bad
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bickley Park Road, Bickley, Greater London

item.title: 10 inch pot hole, 14th March
item.description:
This pot hole has been here for months with no resolution!!!!!!

item.title: Graffiti on street lampost column, 13th March
item.description:

item.title: Hedge overhanging, blocking half of pavement, 13th March
item.description:
Outside 59 Selby Road, the hedge from this property is overhanging pavement, blocking half the pavement. Please could this be looked into with the attached photos.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 59, 59a, Selby Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black "oops" tag on estate map, 13th March
